articles.title: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister reviews heatwave measures and directs relief efforts - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu reviewed the state’s preparedness measures on January 31, 2026, to protect residents from extreme temperatures and heatwave conditions.
To address the heatwave, the Chief Minister instructed officials to provide tents, cooling shelters, cold drinking water, buttermilk, and ORS packets at various locations. He also advised authorities to consider declaring local holidays in areas experiencing severe temperatures and stressed the importance of uninterrupted drinking water supply in both rural and urban regions.
District Collectors were directed to conduct media briefings to raise public awareness regarding heat intensity and necessary precautions. The review meeting was attended by Chief Secretary Sai Prasad and senior officials from the Disaster Management, Health, Municipal Administration, and Panchayat Raj departments.
This directive follows a rise in temperatures across much of India, with several regions experiencing severe heatwaves. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued Red and Orange alerts in multiple states.
Dr Shalini Tyagi, Director of Paediatric Care at Medanta, Noida, advised parents on January 30 to take extra precautions for children amid rising heatwave conditions, highlighting children’s greater vulnerability to dehydration, heat stress, and related infections during extreme weather.
Regarding the impact of heat on children’s health, Dr Tyagi stated, “Every weather change affects children more than it affects adults. If you look at it, children have a larger body surface area relative to their weight, meaning they absorb more heat. Additionally, their thermoregulation system is not as well-developed as it is in adults.”
The IMD has issued an orange alert for heatwave conditions in Delhi, Haryana, and Chandigarh, with similar alerts expected in Madhya Pradesh, Punjab, Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Rajasthan, Telangana, Odisha, and Uttarakhand.
